text: The surgeon uses a surgical device to remove adhesions at the beginning of the operation, allowing for clear visualization of the genital organs.
The surgical field shows no bleeding, demonstrating the efficiency of the surgical device being used.
The surgeon removes the fallopian tubes, carefully dissecting them from surrounding tissue within the abdominal cavity.
The surgeon inspects the normal side, observing the pubic region and confirming the right fallopian tube appears intact and healthy.
The surgeon observes the Faleb Al-Yusra channel, noting a visible load extending along its length, indicating that preservation of this structure is not possible.
The surgeon positions the knife parallel to the Faleb channel, preparing to cut and remove the knife completely from the surgical field.
The surgeon washes the surgical site within the abdominal cavity to ensure there is no bleeding before removing the sample.
The surgeon thoroughly irrigates the pelvic cavity, ensuring all areas are well washed.
The surgeon places the excised sample from the left valve channel into a plastic bag for removal from the abdominal cavity.
